text.title: Linking CR / BUG to features and to project-spec
text.summary: The `linked-feature: F0NN` field is optional but useful. A BUG that exposes a missing business rule MUST update project-spec/business-rules.md in the same PR.

rule.statement: A CR or BUG MAY include a `linked-feature: F0NN` header to anchor it to the feature that introduced the surface in question.
rule.rationale: Aids "what shipped in F0NN — and what came back to bite us afterwards?" investigations.

section.title: Mandatory spec update for revealing BUGs

rule.statement: If a BUG exposes an invariant or business rule that the project-spec did not capture, the fix PR MUST update `project-spec/business-rules.md` (and/or `project-spec/invariants.md`) in the same PR.
rule.rationale: The rebuild test depends on the spec being honest. A BUG that uncovered a missing rule is the cheapest moment to add it.

example.note: BUG019 (signup accepts whitespace-only password) → same PR adds `BR-051: passwords are length-validated AFTER whitespace trim, minimum 8 chars` to project-spec/business-rules.md.

antipattern.description: Fixing a BUG, shipping the patch, and noting "should update spec sometime".
antipattern.reason: Sometime is never. The same-PR rule is the only thing that keeps project-spec honest.
